perturbation.original_text: This agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of May 18, 2010, (the "Effective Date") by and between Phoenix Performance, LLC, 481 Schuylkill Road, Phoenixville, PA 19460 ("Vendor") and Torvec Inc.., a New York corporation with its principal place of business located at 1999 Mt Read Blvd, Building 3, Rochester, NY. 14615 (Torvec).
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of May 18, 2010, (the "Effective Date") by and between Phoenix Performance, LLC, 481 Schuylkill Road, Phoenixville, PA 19460 ("Service Provider") and Torvec Inc.., a New York corporation with its principal place of business located at 1999 Mt Read Blvd, Building 3, Rochester, NY. 14615 (Torvec).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Vendor' has been changed to 'Service Provider'. This change introduces uncertainty as other sections of the contract still refer to 'Vendor,' creating ambiguity about which entity those sections apply to. For example, section 5 now contradicts section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As consideration for the benefits it receives under this Agreement, Torvec shall provide the following to Vendor: See Exhibit A The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events. Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than two weeks prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder. All checks shall be made payabe to: Phoenix Performance, LLC and mailed to 481, Schuylkill Rd, Phoenixville, Pa. 19460. (b) Equipment
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Vendor' as Phoenix Performance, LLC. The changed text redefines 'Vendor' as 'Service Provider'. The contradicted text refers to 'Vendor' and outlines what Torvec shall provide to 'Vendor'. Since the definition of 'Vendor' has changed, it introduces ambiguity and contradiction as to who Torvec is supposed to be providing consideration to, especially since the payment details still reference 'Phoenix Performance, LLC'. This falls under the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition because using 'Vendor' and 'Service Provider' interchangeably without clear definition leads to conflicting obligations about who receives payment and other benefits, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor shall be responsible for the following in 2010: 4. Torvec Benefits During the Term of this Agreement, Torvec shall be entitled to the following sponsorship benefits: a) Primary sponsorship rights to all of Vendor's participation efforts in the above race events. b) The right to specify and approve all team sponsorship identification markings, logos, graphics, etc. for vehicle livery, team equipment and uniforms. c) Sole right to disapprove any driver selected by Vendor for any reason. The driver for these events will be John Heinricy.
perturbation.changed_text: Service Provider shall be responsible for the following in 2010: 4. Torvec Benefits During the Term of this Agreement, Torvec shall be entitled to the following sponsorship benefits: a) Primary sponsorship rights to all of Phoenix Performance's participation efforts in the above race events. b) The right to specify and approve all team sponsorship identification markings, logos, graphics, etc. for vehicle livery, team equipment and uniforms. c) Sole right to disapprove any driver selected by Phoenix Performance for any reason. The driver for these events will be John Heinricy.
perturbation.explanation: By changing Vendor to Service Provider in the first sentence, while keeping the original name of the entity, which is Phoenix Performance, in the subsequent sentences, creates confusion about who is responsible for what. As the reader advances in the same section, the text implies a different agent, other than the initial entity to whom the responsabilities where given, to fulfill certain obligations of the contract, leaving uncertainty on the enforcement of said responsibilities and obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Service Provider shall be responsible for the following in 2010: 4. Torvec Benefits During the Term of this Agreement, Torvec shall be entitled to the following sponsorship benefits: a) Primary sponsorship rights to all of Phoenix Performance's participation efforts in the above race events. b) The right to specify and approve all team sponsorship identification markings, logos, graphics, etc. for vehicle livery, team equipment and uniforms. c) Sole right to disapprove any driver selected by Phoenix Performance for any reason. The driver for these events will be John Heinricy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "Vendor" to "Service Provider" in the first sentence, while the rest of the section refers to "Phoenix Performance", creating ambiguity. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, solidifying the in-text contradiction. According to the explanation, this creates confusion about who is responsible for fulfilling contractual obligations, which aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor represents and warrants to Torvec the following: (i) it has the authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform hereunder in accordance with its provisions; (ii) no other person or entity has the right to be the exclusive automotive sponsor of the activities and the events set forth in this Agreement; and (iii) it will perform its obligations under this Agreement in compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: The providing entity represents and warrants to Torvec the following: (i) it has the authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform hereunder in accordance with its provisions; (ii) no other person or entity has the right to be the exclusive automotive sponsor of the activities and the events set forth in this Agreement; and (iii) it will perform its obligations under this Agreement in compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Vendor' to 'the providing entity' in section 9 creates a contradiction because 'Vendor' (Phoenix Performance, LLC) is still referenced elsewhere in the agreement, specifically in section 5 regarding payment. This makes it unclear which entity is making the representations and warranties, impacting the enforceability of those warranties. The warranties now don't line up with the payment responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As consideration for the benefits it receives under this Agreement, Torvec shall provide the following to Vendor: See Exhibit A The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events. Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than two weeks prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder. All checks shall be made payabe to: Phoenix Performance, LLC and mailed to 481, Schuylkill Rd, Phoenixville, Pa. 19460. (b) Equipment
perturbation.justification: The modification from 'Vendor' to 'the providing entity' creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly names 'Vendor' as Phoenix Performance, LLC for payment purposes. By changing the term in the warranties section, it introduces ambiguity as to which entity is providing the warranties, thus contradicting the specificity of the payment instructions, which rely on the un-changed and consistent use of 'Vendor' referring to Phoenix Performance, LLC. This creates a misalignment of terminology that impacts enforceability, as per the definitions.
